fields.description:
Silent-success detection (truncation-in-a-schema-valid-verdict + conservative finish-reason silent-success) plus two proven output self-heal tweaks on rebar's already-strong structured.py ladder. (The silent-HANG backstop is story hoopoe's per-request/tool timeout — not built here; referenced only.)

## Why
A class of failures never raises — a safety refusal returned AS the answer (pydantic-ai #5221), an empty/truncated result that looks complete (#5769, #3219), a silent hang (#4681) — so exception classification structurally can't see them; they need result validation. Separately, rebar's `structured.py` already implements almost the whole OSS self-heal ladder (native-where-available, tolerant parse, deterministic json-repair BEFORE reask, targeted error-fed reask, fast-fail on truncation/refusal, bounded `OUTPUT_RETRIES=2`) — it needs only two cheap, proven additions.

## What
- Runtime result-validation on BOTH structured output paths (the gap the review found): the PromptedOutput path (`runner.py:519`) already calls `check_stop_reason`, but the **NativeOutput path (`runner.py:497-502`) returns `run_result.output` DIRECTLY with no silent-success check** — this story adds the same `check_stop_reason(getattr(run_result.response,'finish_reason',None))` + refusal-as-content check on the NativeOutput branch before returning. (Anthropic — rebar's provider — uses PromptedOutput today; NativeOutput is the OpenAI-strict-schema path, covered for correctness.) The verdict is validated by the EXISTING `parse_structured` schema validation (already a shape check against the verdict model, not a non-empty heuristic); this story adds the truncation-conjunction + refusal-as-content on top. The per-request/tool timeout from story hoopoe is the silent-hang backstop.
- **Truncation-within-a-schema-valid-verdict:** a schema-shape check alone is insufficient — a truncated response can still deserialize into a valid-but-stripped verdict. So the check is CONJOINED with the finish-reason signal: a verdict that parses BUT whose `finish_reason`/`stop_reason` indicates `length`/`max_tokens` truncation (already surfaced by `check_stop_reason`) is treated as degraded (routed, not returned as PASS/BLOCK), even though it parsed. Schema-valid AND untruncated is the pass condition.
- **Refusal-as-content protocol (concrete, distinct from `check_stop_reason`):** `check_stop_reason` already handles the SDK case where `stop_reason/finish_reason == "refusal"/"content_filter"` with EMPTY parts. The gap (#5221) is a refusal returned as NON-EMPTY content while the provider signals a filter out-of-band. Detection signal — the VERIFIED, structured `finish_reason` ONLY: a refusal / content-filter / truncation normalizes to `content_filter` / `refusal` / `length` on `result.response.finish_reason`, which `check_stop_reason` ALREADY raises `UnretryableOutputError` on. Drake's contribution is (a) running that check on the **NativeOutput path** too (runner.py:497-502 currently returns `run_result.output` with NO stop-reason check — the gap this story closes), so a truncated/refused NativeOutput response — today returned as a hollow verdict — instead raises `UnretryableOutputError` → INDETERMINATE, matching the PromptedOutput path. (There is no 'parses-but-truncated' case to route separately: `check_stop_reason` runs BEFORE the parse, so truncation is caught as the exception, not as a schema-valid-but-stripped verdict.) The harder #5221 case — a refusal returned as NON-EMPTY content with a NORMAL `finish_reason` and only an out-of-band `provider_details` signal — is NOT detected here: its exact Anthropic `provider_details` shape cannot be characterized without a live refusal, so it is DEFERRED IN FULL to a `discovered_from` follow-on ticket (which will observe a real refusal and add the provider-signal probe). This story adds NO speculative provider_details parsing. (Today Anthropic refusals DO normalize to the `finish_reason` the verified signal already catches; the deferred case is the rarer out-of-band one.)

Output self-heal in `src/rebar/llm/structured.py` (EXPLICIT deltas vs current code, both confirmed absent today): (1) `structured.py:161` currently calls `repair_json(cand, return_objects=True)` WITHOUT a `schema=` arg — add `schema=model_cls` (json-repair accepts a pydantic v2 model directly; default `schema_repair_mode='standard'`) for schema-guided deterministic repair before any LLM reask, wrapped in a try/except that falls back to the current schema-less `repair_json` if the schema-guided call raises (so a json-repair edge case never regresses today's behavior). VERIFIED offline (json-repair 0.61.2): `repair_json` params include `schema` (accepts a pydantic model) + `schema_repair_mode: Literal['standard','salvage']='standard'`. Since the current `pyproject.toml` pins `json-repair>=0.30` (where `schema=` may be absent), this story BUMPS the pin to `json-repair>=0.61` so the arg is guaranteed; (2) `runner.py:530`'s reask currently sends `instructions + schema_hint + "Your previous reply could not be parsed/validated ({exc})"` but NOT the raw prior output — add a BOUNDED snippet of the model's faulty prior reply (`str(result.output)[:_FAULTY_OUTPUT_SNIPPET_CHARS]` with `_FAULTY_OUTPUT_SNIPPET_CHARS = 2000` a named module constant, truncated with an ellipsis marker) so the model can diff its own mistake (the LangChain `RetryWithErrorOutputParser` / Instructor pattern). Neither is present today; genuine additions.
- **Schema coordination:** the verdict schema-shape check validates against the verdict schema as OWNED by story 2eca (which adds `resolution_class`/`diagnostic`). This story does NOT edit the verdict JSON schemas — it consumes them; if it lands before 2eca, it checks the pre-existing shape and 2eca's additive fields do not break it.

## Scope
- Runtime checks + the two `structured.py` tweaks. (Cohesion note re: a decomposition suggestion — the NativeOutput stop-reason parity fix and the two self-heal tweaks are ONE cohesive unit: all three are small, same-theme changes to the SAME structured-output reliability path (`runner.py` `_pai_structured` + `structured.py`), share the same tests, and none is independently valuable enough to warrant its own review/CI/land cycle. Keeping them together minimizes integration overhead on a tightly-coupled seam.)
- NOT in scope: a second-interpreter "fixer" LLM (`structured.py` explicitly rejects it as an anti-pattern; keep that stance); verdict-schema edits (owned by story 2eca).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The NativeOutput path runs `check_stop_reason` so a truncated/refused NativeOutput response raises `UnretryableOutputError` → INDETERMINATE (parity with PromptedOutput), instead of returning a hollow verdict — test `test_native_output_path_checks_stop_reason`. Full #5221 refusal-as-content-with-normal-finish_reason is DEFERRED to a follow-on (created during the work; not a close-gate criterion).
- [ ] The verdict is validated by the existing `parse_structured` schema check (no non-empty heuristic to replace); the truncation-conjunction routes degraded results, and a zero-finding CLEAN review still passes untouched (test `test_zero_finding_clean_review_passes`).
- [ ] `repair_json` is called with `schema=model_cls`; the targeted reask includes the bounded faulty prior output — pinned by tests `test_repair_json_receives_schema` and `test_reask_prompt_includes_bounded_prior_output` in `tests/unit/test_silent_success.py`, plus a fallback test that a schema-guided `repair_json` raising falls back to the schema-less call.
- [ ] No second-LLM fixer is introduced; no verdict-schema files are edited by this story.
- [ ] RED silent-success cases flip GREEN; `make test`/`make lint`/`make typecheck` green.

Files: `src/rebar/llm/structured.py`, `src/rebar/llm/runner.py`, `pyproject.toml` (json-repair pin `>=0.30`→`>=0.61`), `tests/unit/test_silent_success.py` (new); read-only on `src/rebar/schemas/*` and the gate verdict-finalization path.

## Verified library behavior (experiment log — pydantic-ai 1.107.0, offline)
ACTUAL captured output (no billable call):
```
ModelResponse fields: [parts, usage, model_name, timestamp, kind, provider_name, provider_url,
                       provider_details, provider_response_id, finish_reason, run_id, ...]
  -> finish_reason present (truncation/refusal signal, read by structured.check_stop_reason)
  -> provider_details present (the STRUCTURED out-of-band filter signal drake keys on, NOT a regex)
rebar structured.check_stop_reason(stop_reason: str | None) -> None   (reused for the truncation conjunction)
```
json-repair schema-guided repair, VERIFIED offline:
```
json-repair 0.61.2: repair_json params = [json_str, return_objects, skip_json_loads, logging, json_fd,
    chunk_length, stream_stable, strict, schema, schema_repair_mode, json_dumps_args]  -> schema + schema_repair_mode present
```
The current pin `json-repair>=0.30` predates the `schema=` arg; this story bumps it to `>=0.61` (0.61.2 is
already installed, so the bump is a floor-raise, not a runtime change). So the refusal-as-content detection keys on the structured provider signal (finish_reason +
provider_details), never a regex over answer text; and the truncation conjunction reuses the existing
check_stop_reason. A detected silent-success is routed through story mamba's shared LLMOutcome
(resolution_class=CHANGE_INPUT + sanitized diagnostic) — one routing path, not a second. These are
pydantic-ai LIBRARY response fields, not rebar symbols (bug succinct-formable-kite: repo-absence !=
non-existence). RED baseline (arawana, committed) pins today: canned content_filter/length finish_reason
-> UnretryableOutputError; text mode does NOT read finish_reason (the silent-success gap drake closes).
